title:
Lesson 1:
Sure / Of Course / With Pleasure
description: 'Tabii' = sure / of course. 'Tabii ki' = of course (stronger). 'Memnuniyetle' = with pleasure. 'Seve seve' = gladly. These express willingness from casual to formal.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Bana bu konuda yardım edebilir misin?
dialogue.english: Can you help me with this?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Emin misin? Seni rahatsız etmek istemiyorum.
dialogue.english: Are you sure? I don't want to bother you.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Of course. No problem at all.

title:
Lesson 2:
Please / Excuse Me / I'm Sorry
description: 'Lütfen' = please. 'Affedersiniz' = excuse me (formal, getting attention). 'Üzgünüm' = I'm sorry (expressing regret). Each has a specific role in polite conversation.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Excuse me, where is the bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm sorry, I don't know. I'm not from here.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Sorun değil. Lütfen, başka biri yardım edebilir mi?
dialogue.english: No problem. Please, can someone else help?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm very sorry. I hope you find it.

title:
Lesson 3:
It Doesn't Matter
description: 'Önemli değil' = It's not important / It doesn't matter. 'Fark etmez' = It makes no difference / Doesn't matter. 'Fark etmez' is more casual; 'önemli değil' is more explanatory.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: It doesn't matter. We're still waiting for the others.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I also forgot to bring the documents.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Bugün için önemli değil. Bir dahaki sefere getirirsin.
dialogue.english: It's not important for today. You can bring them next time.

vocabulary.english: it's not important / it doesn't matter
it makes no difference / doesn't matter
